page: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of RAM, providing 5.62 GB of memory per core. Storage resources are a key feature of this instance, consisting of 7650 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile characterized by moderate multi-threaded capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. The single-core performance is categorized as weak to poor, with stress-ng single-core in the bottom 25% and Geekbench single-core in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, also fall into the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is more robust; the instance achieves a strong rating in the top 25% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and average ratings in the middle 50% across stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, consistently perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i1-180 offers high local storage density and dedicated physical cores, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial NVMe capacity without demanding top-tier single-core compute performance. The trade-off lies in the older CPU architecture, which limits single-threaded efficiency but provides stable, dedicated multi-core resources. This server is suited for storage-heavy workloads, database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving.
description: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs at 2.2 GHz. It is equipped with 180.0 GB of RAM and 7650 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct trade-off: single-core performance is weak, with Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression, reach the top 25%. Most multi-core, memory, and database benchmarks fall into the average tier. Given its high storage capacity and dedicated core allocation, this instance is suited for storage-intensive applications, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks where massive local NVMe storage is required.
og_description: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 180 GB RAM, and 7650 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering average multi-core performance for database and storage workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel cores, 180 GB RAM, and 7650 GB NVMe storage, suited for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated cores and 7.6 TB of NVMe storage for data-intensive, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel physical cores and vCPUs running at 2.2 GHz
180.0 GB of system memory providing 5.62 GB per core
7650 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data needs
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Strong multi-threaded decompression performance offset by weak single-core speeds
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and storage-heavy applications
